speaker: rep
text: Hi David, thanks for hopping on again. Last time we walked through the platform and you seemed interested in moving forward.

speaker: prospect
text: Yeah, the product looks solid. But I need to talk about pricing before we go any further.

speaker: rep
text: Absolutely, happy to discuss. What's on your mind?

speaker: prospect
text: Honestly, the price is too expensive for what we're getting. $149 per seat for 15 reps is over $26,000 a year.

speaker: rep
text: I hear you, that's a real concern. Let me ask though - what's the cost of your current low reply rates? At 3%, how many meetings is your team missing per month?

speaker: prospect
text: I mean, we're probably leaving a lot on the table. But I still have to justify the budget to my VP.

speaker: rep
text: That makes total sense. Let's, um, like, sort of frame it in terms of ROI so you have something concrete to bring to your VP.

speaker: prospect
text: Sure, but I've seen vendors inflate their ROI numbers. What's realistic?

speaker: rep
text: Fair point. So our median customer at your size sees reply rates go from roughly 3% to 9% within 90 days. With your volume of 10,000 emails a month, that's roughly 600 extra replies turning into about 40-50 additional meetings per quarter.

speaker: prospect
text: 40-50 extra meetings would be significant. But what if it doesn't work for us?

speaker: rep
text: We actually offer a 90-day performance guarantee. If you don't see at least a 2x improvement in reply rates, we'll extend your contract at no cost until you do.

speaker: prospect
text: That's interesting. I didn't know about the guarantee. What about a discount for annual commitment?

speaker: rep
text: So basically for annual we can do 20% off which brings it down to about $119 per seat and then there's also the startup discount and the growth package and we have add-ons for analytics and AI features and premium support and custom integrations and there's implementation fees too.

speaker: prospect
text: Okay, too many options. Just tell me what the annual price would be for 15 seats on the growth tier.

speaker: rep
text: Right, sorry. 15 seats at $119 per seat annually would be $21,420 per year, billed annually.

speaker: prospect
text: That's still a lot. We're also looking at a competitor that's about 30% cheaper.

speaker: rep
text: Yeah, um, so, like, I think maybe we're just, you know, sort of better because our AI is more advanced.

speaker: prospect
text: Can you be more specific about what makes your AI better?

speaker: rep
text: Good question. Our model is trained on 50 million sales interactions specifically. The cheaper alternatives typically use generic language models. The difference shows up in personalization quality - our customers report that AI-generated intros pass the 'human test' 85% of the time versus about 40% for competitors.

speaker: prospect
text: Those numbers are compelling if they're real. Can you connect me with a reference customer in our industry?

speaker: rep
text: Absolutely. I have two fintech companies similar to your size who'd be happy to chat. I'll set that up this week.

speaker: prospect
text: Perfect. If the reference checks out and we can lock in that annual rate, I think we can make this work.

speaker: rep
text: Great, I'll send over the reference intros today and a formal quote with the annual pricing. Should we schedule a follow-up for next Tuesday?

speaker: prospect
text: Tuesday works. Morning is better for me.

speaker: rep
text: I'll send a calendar invite for 10 AM. Thanks David, talk to you Tuesday.
